examples.title: False Sharing Prone Parallel Histogram
examples.description: Parallel histogram computation without padding - susceptible to false sharing
examples.explanation: Each thread increments its assigned bin, but bins[0], bins[1], etc. are adjacent in memory and likely share the same cache line. When thread 0 increments bin[0] and thread 1 increments bin[1], the cache line containing both gets invalidated repeatedly, causing severe performance degradation despite no true data dependency.

examples.title: False Sharing Free Histogram with Padding
examples.description: Optimized parallel histogram using cache line padding to eliminate false sharing
examples.explanation: Padding each counter to 64 bytes ensures each histogram bin resides on its own cache line. When different threads update different bins, no cache line invalidation occurs because each thread's target is on a separate line. This eliminates the false sharing problem at the cost of more memory.

examples.title: Thread-Local Accumulation Pattern
examples.description: Using thread-private variables to avoid false sharing with manual reduction
examples.explanation: Two approaches to avoid false sharing: (1) Thread-local accumulation where each thread writes to its own private array element (local_sums[tid]), then combines at the end with minimal atomic operations. (2) Using OpenMP's reduction clause which automatically creates private copies and combines them. Both avoid the false sharing that would occur if all threads wrote to the same shared 'sum' variable.
